name: Lantern Arc Reactor MK1 ASI-Integrated Confidence Model
meaning: ASI-aware proof-weighted confidence scorecard for moving from garage prototype through public platform to human trial demo readiness with strict claim boundaries.
currentPhase: Movie 1 garage proven; Movie 2 proof loop active; ASI patterns integrated as architecture references only; comprehensive documentation and reporting complete
confidenceRule: Scores rise only from evidence receipts, observed outcomes, repeated validation, paid/user proof, canary-passed automation, and Brier-style error tracking. ASI patterns are architecture references, not capability claims.
raisesMovie2: 5 outreach sends recorded in the wallet ledger
1 paid pilot or hard rejection batch
1 public proof page or demo artifact
Discord lounge bot health check passes without leaking secrets
MCP canary validates actual exposed tools before any command execution
D: shrunk and dual boot readyForInstall=true
one workflow used by someone other than the operator
raisesHumanTrial: 5 successful $1000 founding seat demos with cleared cash
MCP canary validates all exposed tools before automation
Documented rollback path for all automated actions
Explicit human approval recorded for each trial participant
Certified PPE or tested prototype evidence if medical claims
No ASI capability claim without independent validation
Brier-style error tracking with forecast/outcome pairs
Safety automation gates configured and tested

asiPatternBoundaries.coordinationGovernance: architecture reference only; no local ASI capability claim
asiPatternBoundaries.decentralizedCompute: infrastructure reference; no free/invisible cloud compute claim
asiPatternBoundaries.agentNetworks: collective intelligence framing; no bypass human approval claim
asiPatternBoundaries.tokenGovernance: boundary reference; no investment advice claim

calibrationProtocol: Forecast before action with confidence interval
Score miss/hit with Brier-style error thinking
Update confidence only after observed evidence
ASI patterns remain architecture references, not capability claims
boundaries: No Discord-to-MCP command execution before canary
No v1.0.0 tag without operator approval
No store fee until product target is chosen
No medical or PPE claim without evidence
No ASI capability claim without independent validation
No token issuance or investment advice
No human trial readiness without cleared cash and safety gates
No automated action without MCP canary validation
